title: Beehiiv vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for newsletters vs e-commerce.
intro: Beehiiv and Drip are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Beehiiv is built for newsletter creators and publishers seeking monetization opportunities, while Drip specializes in e-commerce email marketing with deep store integration. Choosing between them depends on your business model and primary use case.

comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce businesses with deep store integration needs
Visual automation builder with advanced workflows

choose_a_if: You're launching a newsletter or publication and want to monetize through ads and sponsorships
You're starting out and need a free tier with room to grow to 2,500 subscribers
You value built-in audience engagement features like web reader and referral programs
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need revenue attribution and purchase tracking
You want advanced automation workflows with a visual builder for complex customer journeys
You need SMS capabilities in addition to email marketing

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Beehiiv scores higher overall (4.7 vs 4.4 on G2) and offers better value for newsletter publishers with its free tier and monetization features. However, Drip is purpose-built for e-commerce and offers more sophisticated automation and revenue tracking that's essential for online stores.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Drip?
faq.a: Beehiiv is significantly cheaper because it offers a free tier supporting up to 2,500 subscribers with no payment required. Drip has no free option and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious publishers, Beehiiv is the clear winner on price.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support CSV exports of your subscriber lists and email content, making migration possible. However, you'll need to manually recreate any complex automation workflows in Beehiiv's builder, and note that Beehiiv's strengths are in newsletter publishing rather than e-commerce features.
